meta.verdictHeadline: Behind schedule — but on the predicted path
meta.verdictSub: Reality is running at roughly 65–75% of the pace the AI 2027 scenario predicted. The story is holding; the clock is slipping.

tiles.label: Observed pace
tiles.note: of the scenario's predicted speed, on quantitative metrics (authors' own grading).

tiles.label: Takeoff window
tiles.note: Full-coding-automation → superintelligence, re-projected at the observed pace.

tiles.label: Qualitative calls
tiles.note: Agents arrived on cue; coding agents became real tools. It's the benchmark numbers that lag.

milestones.label: Stumbling agents
milestones.tip: Mid-2025: 'Stumbling agents' — personal-assistant agents debut, struggle for adoption.

milestones.label: Agent-1-scale run
milestones.tip: Late 2025: 10^27-FLOP frontier model ('Agent-1'), biggest datacenters ever built.

milestones.label: Superhuman coder
milestones.tip: Mar 2027: superhuman coder — AI fully automates AI research engineering.

milestones.label: Superintelligence
milestones.tip: Late 2027: takeoff completes — superintelligence in the scenario's race branch.

band.label: Revised takeoff window
band.tip: Authors' re-projection (AI Futures Model, adjusted for compute/labor slowdowns): takeoff runs mid-2028 to mid-2030.

milestones.label: Agents arrived ✓
milestones.tip: Confirmed: ChatGPT Agent-style assistants shipped mid/late 2025 and struggled for adoption — almost exactly as written.

milestones.label: Frontier-scale run
milestones.tip: Not yet: no confirmed training run substantially beyond GPT-4.5 as of Jul 2026.

milestones.label: Superhuman coder
milestones.tip: Superhuman coder, re-projected: around mid-2028 at observed pace. Authors' own medians: 2029 (Kokotajlo) to early 2030s (Lifland).

milestones.label: Superintelligence
milestones.tip: Superintelligence, re-projected: ~2030 at observed pace with the authors' slowdown adjustments.

paceMetrics.label: OpenAI revenue
paceMetrics.tip: Annualized revenue hit ~$20B against a predicted $18B — slightly ahead of the scenario.

paceMetrics.label: METR coding time horizon
paceMetrics.tip: Agents' 80%-reliability coding time horizon is moving at 1.04× the central AI-2027 trajectory — on pace.

paceMetrics.label: All quantitative metrics
paceMetrics.tip: Aggregate across all quantitative predictions: ~65% pace in Feb 2026, revised to ~75% with July 2026 data.

paceMetrics.label: Company valuation
paceMetrics.tip: OpenAI reached the predicted $500B valuation ~4 months late — 'well behind pace' per the authors. Multiplier is an estimate.

paceMetrics.label: SWE-Bench Verified
paceMetrics.tip: Predicted 85% by mid-2025 from a 72% baseline; best actual was 74.5% (Opus 4.1) — about a fifth of the predicted gain. Estimate.

paceNote: Revenue, METR, and the aggregate are the authors' own figures; valuation and SWE-Bench multipliers are estimated here from their reported predicted-vs-actual values. Not shown for lack of a defensible number: AI R&D uplift (behind) and largest-training-run compute (behind, high uncertainty).

scorecard.said: Mid-2025; "order me a burrito"-style tasks; weak adoption
scorecard.reality: ChatGPT Agent (Jul 2025) et al.; weak adoption

scorecard.said: Autonomous coders taking instructions via chat, saving hours or days
scorecard.reality: Happened — the scenario's biggest qualitative call landed

scorecard.said: Meaningful speedup of AI research by end of 2025
scorecard.reality: End-2025 uplift ≈ what was predicted for the start of 2027's run-up
No confirmed run substantially beyond GPT-4.5

verdictCards.title: Not on schedule
verdictCards.body: Reality runs at roughly two-thirds to three-quarters of the scenario's speed. Held constant, the 2027 takeoff slides to mid-2028 → mid-2030 — and the authors' own medians (2029 to early 2030s for full coding automation) already sit past the title year. 2027 was their modal year, never their median.

verdictCards.title: Directionally accurate
verdictCards.body: As a map of what happens in what order — agents first, then coding automation, then a compute-fueled race — it's tracking remarkably well, and the authors grade themselves publicly. Treat it as a well-built scenario with a sliding clock, not a countdown.

verdictCards.title: One anomaly worth watching
verdictCards.body: Benchmarks are lagging the qualitative shifts, when they'd normally lead them. That suggests the recursive AI-R&D speedup the dramatic endings depend on is kicking in slower than modeled — the scenario's most contested assumption.

sources.label: Grading AI 2027's 2025 Predictions — AI Futures Project

changelog.entry: AGI Watch launches. Baseline: authors' Feb 2026 scorecard (~65% pace) plus their July 2026 preliminary update (~75%). Takeoff window re-projected to mid-2028 → mid-2030.
